topic.para: The &qemu; guest agent (GA) runs inside the &vmguest; and allows the &vmhost; to run commands in the guest operating system via &libvirt;. It supports many functions—for example, getting details about guest file systems, freezing and thawing file systems, or suspending or rebooting a guest.
&qemu; GA is included in the qemu-guest-agent package and is installed, configured and activated by default on &kvm; virtual machines.

section.title: Running &qemu; GA commands
section.para: &qemu; GA includes many built-in commands that do not have direct &libvirt; counterparts. Refer to the &qemu; GA documentation to find the complete list. You can run all the &qemu; GA commands by using &libvirt;'s general purpose command qemu-agent-command:

section.title: &virsh; commands that require &qemu; GA
section.para: Several &virsh; commands require &qemu; GA for their functionality. For example, the following ones:

listitem.para: Prints information about the guest from the guest's point of view.
Queries or changes the state of virtual CPUs from the guest's point of view.
Sets the password for a user account in the guest.
Shows a list of mounted file systems within the running domain.

section.title: Enhancing &libvirt; commands
section.para: If &qemu; GA is enabled inside the guest, several &virsh; subcommands have enhanced functionality when run in the agent mode. The following list includes only certain examples of them. For a complete list, see the &virsh; man page and search for the agent string.

varlistentry.term: virsh shutdown --mode agent and virsh reboot --mode agent

listitem.para: This method of shutting down or rebooting leaves the guest clean for its next run, similar to the ACPI method.
Instructs the guest to make its file system quiescent—to flush all I/O operations in the cache and leave volumes in a consistent state, so that no checks are needed when they are remounted.
Changes the number of CPUs assigned to a guest.
Queries the &qemu; GA for the guest's IP address.
Prints information about the virtual CPU counts from the perspective of the guest.
A complete list of commands supported by the &qemu; GA is at .
The &virsh; man page (man 1 virsh) includes descriptions of the commands that support the &qemu; GA interface.
